creator: hcoin@quietfountain.com
text:
> These options are presented through a dialog screen by default, unless
> you specifically turn them off through BATCH or other advanced flags.

I see where we are miscommunicating.    What happens when one tries it 
through the means used in most places that don't want to be developers, 
that just want to deploy official releases?  They (we) use pkg_add -r  
whatnot.   The canonical new release upgrade tools.   Packages that 
require to work together in such a major way ought not force users to 
comprehend 'make config' and the inner life of the developer's 
options.   Can't the default used to create the packages just include 
the necessary?  Or perhaps two package versions with suitable dependencies?

Harry


--
